title: Meta stock sinks after Q1 earnings as company raises 2026 AI spending forecast to $125 billion-$145 billion
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: Meta stock fell 10% after Q1 earnings, with the company raising its 2026 AI spending forecast to $125-$145 billion. The stock dropped 8% in after-hours trading
excerpt: Meta stock fell 10% after Q1 earnings, with the company raising its 2026 AI spending forecast to $125-$145 billion. The stock dropped 8% in after-hours trading following the earnings report.

content.introduction: Meta stock fell 10% after Q1 earnings, with the company raising its 2026 AI spending forecast to $125-$145 billion. The stock dropped 8% in after-hours trading following the earnings report.
body: TLDR META stock dropped ~8% in after-hours trading after Q1 earnings Q1 EPS came in at $10.44 on revenue of $56.3 billion, beating Wall Street estimates 2026 capexTLDR META stock dropped ~8% in after-hours trading after Q1 earnings Q1 EPS came in at $10.44 on revenue of $56.3 billion, beating Wall Street estimates 2026 capex. # Meta Platforms (META) Stock Falls After Raising 2026 AI Spending to $145 Billion. For feedback or concerns regarding this content, please contact us at crypto.news@mexc.com. * META stock dropped ~8% in after-hours trading after Q1 earnings. * Q1 EPS came in at $10.44 on revenue of $56.3 billion, beating Wall Street estimates. * Daily active users across Meta’s platforms rose 4% year-over-year to 3.56 billion. Meta now expects 2026 capex to land between $125 billion and $145 billion, up from its previous range of $115 billion to $135 billion. The post Meta Platforms (META) Stock Falls After Raising 2026 AI Spending to $145 Billion appeared first on CoinCentral.
Meta stock was down about 6% in after-hours trading following the results. Meta said its 2026 capex spending will come in between $125 billion

Zuckerberg [told analysts](https://www.investors.com/news/technology/meta-stock-q4-ai-spending/) in January that the company expects to spend $115 billion to $135 billion on 2026 capital expenditures. That's after spending about $70 billion in 2025, a total that was [revised higher by Meta](https://www.investors.com/news/technology/meta-stock-q3-2025-earnings-ai-meta-news-zuckerberg/) throughout the year. On the [stock market today](https://www.investors.com/news/stock-market-today-stock-market-news/?), Meta stock dropped more than 7% to 616 premarket. *   ##### [Stock Market Today: Dow Adds 700 Points; Meta Falls, Lilly Up 10% As AI Data Centers Lead Gains (Live Coverage)](https://www.investors.com/market-trend/stock-market-today/dow-jones-sp500-nasdaq-inflation-data-meta-stock-meta-earnings/). *   ##### [Meta Stock Edges Lower With Q1 Results Due.
